name: Imperial 150th Anniversary White Cake Classic
recipeInstructions:
Cream the butter until soft and light. Continue creaming several minutes to incorporate as much air as possible.
Gradually add sugar, continuing to cream for an additional 5-7 minutes.
Sift flour, baking powder, and salt together three times.
Alternately add milk and water to the creamed mixture, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients, beating well after each addition.
Add almond flavoring.
In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry. Fold into batter gently, blending thoroughly without overmixing.
Divide batter evenly among three greased and floured 9-inch layer pans.
Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool on racks for 5 minutes before turning cakes onto wire racks to cool completely.
Frost with Chocolate Frosting (see below).
Chocolate Frosting:
Melt chocolate over hot water, stirring constantly. Allow to cool slightly.
In a bowl, combine powdered sugar, salt, and melted butter until smooth.
Add vanilla extract and incorporate fully.
Adjust the frosting's color and flavor by adding more melted chocolate if desired.
Spread evenly over cooled cake layers.
This recipe yields a 9-inch 3-layer white cake.
